UT Austin Invitational 2022

Remote Sensing C — Science Olympiad Practice Quiz

52 Questions · Sections A–E (D & E Simplified)

Section A: Multiple Choice (Q1–Q23)
Use your general knowledge to choose the best answer. Some questions require selecting more than one answer (marked with checkboxes). Questions 15–23 refer to the refraction diagram below.
Q1 (2 points)
Does climate change exist?
Q2 (2 points) — Select all that apply
Which of the following gases contribute to climate change?
Select more than 1
Q3 (2 points) — Select all that apply
Which probes from the rules are sun-synchronous?
Select more than 1
Q4 (2 points) — Select all that apply
Which probes from the rules are part of the A-Train?
Select more than 1
Q5 (2 points) — Select all that apply
Which probes are used to measure "Atmospheric and Sea Surface Temperatures"?
Select more than 1
Q6 (2 points) — Select all that apply
Which probes are used to create "infrared and Doppler radar imagery of clouds and precipitation"?
Select more than 1
Q7 (2 points) — Select all that apply
Which probes are used to measure "aerosol scattering, absorption, and optical depth"?
Select more than 1
Q8 (2 points) — Select all that apply
Which probes are used to "detect trace gas concentrations"?
Select more than 1
Q9 (2 points) — Select all that apply
Which probes are used to measure "sea level rise and surface waves"?
Select more than 1
Q10 (2 points)
What type of scattering occurs when λ ≈ r (wavelength and size of particle respectively)?
Q11 (2 points)
What type of scattering occurs when λ ≫ r (wavelength and size of particle respectively)?
Q12 (2 points)
What type of scattering occurs when λ ≪ r (wavelength and size of particle respectively)?
Q13 (2 points)
Which of the following is closest to the force of gravity on the surface of the Earth?
Q14 (2 points)
Which of the following is closest to the force of gravity on the surface of the Moon, given that the Moon is ≈ 1/6 the mass of Earth?
Refraction Diagram — n₁, n₂, and n₃ are refractive indices
Q15 (2 points)
Which out of n₁, n₂, and n₃ is the largest?
Use the refraction diagram shown above. n₁, n₂, and n₃ are refractive indices.
Q16 (2 points)
Which out of n₁, n₂, and n₃ is the smallest?
Use the refraction diagram shown above. n₁, n₂, and n₃ are refractive indices.
Q17 (2 points)
Assuming the medium with highest refractive index was glass and the medium with the lowest refractive index was air, which would be the material of the remaining medium?
Q18 (2 points)
In which of n₁, n₂, and n₃ is the wavelength of a given wave the shortest?
Q19 (2 points)
In which of n₁, n₂, and n₃ is the wavelength of a given wave the longest?
Q20 (2 points)
In which of n₁, n₂, and n₃ is the frequency of a given wave the smallest?
Q21 (2 points)
In which of n₁, n₂, and n₃ is the frequency of a given wave the largest?
Q22 (2 points)
In which of n₁, n₂, and n₃ is the speed of a given wave the smallest?
Q23 (2 points)
In which of n₁, n₂, and n₃ is the speed of a given wave the smallest?
Section B: GOES-16 (Q24–Q46)
Use Figures 1–9 to answer the following questions. For short-answer questions, type your response. Click "Show Answer" to reveal the correct answer.
Figure 1 - Geostationary Lightning Mapper Flash Extent Density
Figure 2 - Sandwich RGB
Figure 3 - Day Cloud Phase RGB
Figure 4 - Fire Temperature
Figure 5 - "Veggie" Band
Figure 6 - Near IR Cirrus
Figure 7 - IR Shortwave
Figure 8a - Longwave (a)
Figure 8b - Longwave (b)
Figure 8c - Longwave (c)
Figure 9 - CO2 Longwave IR Band
Q24 (2 points) [SLA]
What date were these images taken on?
Q25 (2 points) [Short]
What hemisphere are these images taken in?
Q26 (5 points) [SLA]
How does the Coriolis Effect support your above answer?
Q27 (3 points) [Short]
Which of the figure(s) show channel(s) that are only usable during daytime?
Q28 (5 points) [SLA]
What property do these figures share that made you select them for the previous question?
Q29 (3 points) [Short]
Which of the figure(s) show channel(s) that are passive sensors?
Q30 (5 points) [SLA]
What makes these channel(s) passive (i.e., what is the definition of a passive sensor)?
Q31 (3 points) [Short]
Which of the figure(s) show channel(s) that measure cloud convention?
Q32 (5 points) [SLA]
At what time of day are these measurements less accurate?
Q33 (3 points) [Short]
Which figure(s) are used to calculate Earth's Energy Budget?
Q34 (5 points) [SLA]
What do the figure(s) show that is advantageous for determining the Earth's energy budget (i.e., list what the figure(s) show)?
Q35 (3 points) [Short]
Which of the figure(s) can be used to determine the locations of the clouds or thunderstorms?
Q36 (5 points) [SLA]
How does Figure 1 help determine the location of thunderstorms (i.e., what do the bright spots represent)? Does this have high or low spatial resolution? High or low temporal resolution?
Q37 (3 points) [Short]
Which of the figure(s) show measurements that are interrupted by water vapor (include those which intend to measure water vapor)?
Q38 (5 points) [SLA]
What property of water vapor causes this interruption in just the set of figure(s) above and not all of the figure(s)? (Hint: refer to a chemical property of water)
Q39 (3 points) [Short]
Which of the figure(s) show measurements that can help determine the height of cloud tops?
Q40 (5 points) [SLA]
What optical property of clouds does GOES-16 rely on to determine cloud heights based on these measurements?
Q41 (22 points) [LA]
Which band(s) of GOES-16 are needed to generate the measurements shown by each Figure (1-9)? List them by Figure (e.g., Figure 1 - 1, 2, 3; Figure 2 - 1, etc.)
Q42 (6 points) [Short]
Figures 8a, 8b, and 8c measure the same phenomenon using three different bands which result in different spatial qualities ("dirty", "clean", "regular"). Match 8a, 8b, and 8c to their respective qualities.
Q43 (12 points) [LA]
Explain why you assigned Figures 8a, 8b, and 8c as you did in the previous question.
Q44 (3 points) [Short]
Figure 5 is showing measurements from the band known as the "Veggie" Band. What is the numerical name of the band referenced above?
Q45 (3 points) [LA]
Why is the band referenced in the previous question nicknamed the "Veggie" Band (i.e., what does it help measure that gives it that nickname)?
Q46 (5 points) [LA]
What does Figure 4 imply about Southern California? How do you know?
Section C: Case Study 1 — Where to Live (Q47–Q52)
Use Figures 10–14 to explain your choices. You are searching for a new place to live in the United States based on remote sensing data.
Figure 10 - Groundwater
Figure 11a - Sea Level A
Figure 11b - Sea Level B
Figure 12 - Carbon Concentrations
Figure 13 - Chemical A Concentrations
Figure 14 - Chemical B Concentrations

U.S. Map Regions:

A - Pacific   B - Rocky Mountains   C - Midwest   D - Northeast

E - Southwest   F - Southeast   G - Non-contiguous

Requirements for choosing where to live:

1. Must have a stable source of water   2. Must not be in danger of drastic sea level changes   3. Must minimize noxious chemicals

Q47 — Based on Figure 10 (Groundwater)
(a) (2 pts) What probe measured the information present in the Figure?
(b) (3 pts) How does the probe measure the quantity measured in the Figure?
(c) (5 pts) What region of the United States satisfies the conditions based on the Figure?
Q48 — Based on Figures 11a and 11b (Sea Level)
(a) What probe measured the information present in Figure 11a? (2 pts)
(b) How does the probe measure the quantity measured in Figure 11a? (3 pts)
(c) What is the name of the phenomenon present in Figure 11a? (2 pts)
(d) What is the cause of the phenomenon present in Figure 11a? (5 pts)
(e) What probe measured the information present in Figure 11b? (2 pts)
(f) How does the probe measure the quantity measured in Figure 11b? (3 pts)
(g) What is the name of the phenomenon present in Figure 11b? (2 pts)
(h) What is the cause of the phenomenon present in Figure 11b? (5 pts)
(i) What region of the United States satisfies the conditions based on the Figure? (6 pts)
Q49 — Based on Figure 12 (Carbon Concentrations)
(a) What probe measured the information present in the Figure? (2 pts)
(b) How does the probe measure the quantity measured in the Figure? (3 pts)
(c) What region of the United States satisfies the conditions based on the Figure? (5 pts)
Q50 — Based on Figure 13 (Chemical A Concentrations)
(a) What is "Chemical A" (the chemical whose concentration is being measured)? (5 pts)
(b) What probe measured the information present in the Figure? (2 pts)
(c) How does the probe measure the quantity measured in the Figure? (3 pts)
(d) What region of the United States satisfies the conditions based on the Figure? (5 pts)
Q51 — Based on Figure 14 (Chemical B Concentrations)
(a) What is "Chemical B" (the chemical whose concentration is being measured)? (5 pts)
(b) What probe measured the information present in the Figure? (2 pts)
(c) How does the probe measure the quantity measured in the Figure? (3 pts)
(d) What region of the United States satisfies the conditions based on the Figure? (5 pts)
Q52 (25 points) [LA]
Based on your answers to the other questions from this section, what is the optimal region for you to move to? Explain your reasoning.
Section D: Planetary Equilibrium Temperature (Simplified)
Use the planetary equilibrium temperature equation to answer the following conceptual questions. (Calculus-heavy questions removed.)
I₀(π)(R²p)(1 − a) = 4πR²pσT⁴planet
Q53 (10 points) [LA]
Solve the equation above for Tplanet.
Q54 (20 points) [LA]
Explain, qualitatively, the dependence of the planetary equilibrium temperature on its parameters (i.e., why does the equation depend on the parameters in the equations)?
Q56 (2 points) [Short]
If the albedo (a) of a planet increases, does the planetary equilibrium temperature decrease or increase?
Section E: Blackbody Radiation Concepts (Simplified)
Conceptual questions about blackbody radiation and photon energy. Use Figure A below. (Integral and derivation questions removed.)
Figure A - Blackbody Radiation
Q63 — Use the given diagram of blackbody radiation (Figure A)
(a) What is the parameter being graphed on the x-axis labeled 1? (6 pts)
(c) Graphs A, B, and C are all graphs of the same blackbody at different temperatures. Order A, B, and C from highest temperature to lowest temperature. (3 pts)
(e) What is the term for the divergence between Graphs A, B, and C and Graph D? (5 pts)
Q64 — Photon Energy
Constants: h = 6.626 × 10⁻³⁴, c = 2.998 × 10⁸, k = 1.380649 × 10⁻²³
(a) What is the energy of a photon of frequency f? (5 pts)

Based on the UT Austin Invitational 2022 — Remote Sensing C
